summary: Joel announces that Fog Creek Software is now accepting summer internship applications with a changed process. Previously, they waited until a February deadline to review all applications at once for fairness, but this meant top candidates had already accepted offers elsewhere (notably Microsoft). This year they're switching to a first-come, first-served review process, urging students to apply immediately rather than wait.
key_points: Batch-processing applications for fairness cost them top candidates who accepted other offers while waiting
Speed of hiring decisions matters more than procedural fairness when competing for talent
Switching to rolling/first-come-first-served admissions to avoid losing candidates to competitors like Microsoft
money_quotes: It was fair, but it also meant that by the time we even looked at the resumes, some great applicants had already accepted summer jobs at Microsoft.
If you're a college student looking for an internship, you pretty much should just apply now, instead of puttering around indecisively.
key_insight: Optimizing a hiring process for fairness at the expense of speed can cost you the best candidates to faster-moving competitors.
